title: rubus fruticosus subsp. suberectus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. suberectus, also known as the blackberry or bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Rubus fruticosus subsp. suberectus include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of consuming Rubus fruticosus subsp. suberectus include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Rubus fruticosus subsp. suberectus include tea, jam, and tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
